subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the basic laws of reflection and refraction
Describe image formation by plane mirrors, spherical mirrors, and lenses
Apply mirror formula, lens formula, and magnification with correct sign convention
Explain refractive index, apparent depth, and common optical phenomena
Perform simple experiments to verify key concepts
Relate chapter concepts to everyday optical devices and applications

lesson_plan.title: Reflection – Plane Mirrors and Laws
teaching_strategy: Demonstration using plane mirror and laser/torch
Interactive board work on ray diagrams
student_activities: Draw ray diagram for plane mirror image location
Perform activity to observe lateral inversion
List 5 daily life uses of plane mirrors

lesson_plan.title: Spherical Mirrors – Types and Image Formation
topics_covered: Key terms: pole, centre of curvature, focus
Image formation by concave mirror (different positions)
Uses of concave and convex mirrors
student_activities: Draw ray diagrams for at least 4 object positions in concave mirror
Compare images in concave vs convex mirror using actual mirrors
Make a table of image characteristics
assessment: Accuracy of ray diagrams and table

lesson_plan.title: Mirror Formula, Magnification and Refraction Basics
topics_covered: Introduction to refraction and bending of light
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of refraction using glass slab
Explanation with real examples (straw in water)
student_activities: Solve 5-6 simple numerical problems on mirror formula and magnification
Trace ray through glass slab and note lateral shift
Calculate refractive index from given data

lesson_plan.title: Lenses – Types, Image Formation and Formula
teaching_strategy: Demonstration using convex lens and screen
student_activities: Find approximate focal length of convex lens using distant object
Draw ray diagrams for convex lens (3 positions)
Solve numericals on lens formula and power
assessment: Ray diagrams and focal length observation

lesson_plan.title: Applications and Common Phenomena
topics_covered: Apparent depth and why objects appear raised in water
Uses of lenses and mirrors in daily life
Human eye defects (myopia, hypermetropia) – corrective lenses
teaching_strategy: Demonstration of apparent depth with coin in beaker
student_activities: Perform apparent depth activity and calculate refractive index
Make a poster/chart showing 6 optical devices with ray diagrams
Explain how spectacles correct vision defects

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Worksheet and Application Questions
topics_covered: Recap of key formulas, ray diagrams, and concepts
student_activities: Write one real-life example per concept

teaching_aids: Plane mirror, concave & convex mirrors, convex & concave lenses
Laser pointer or narrow beam torch
Glass slab, beaker with water & coin
Ray diagram charts and NCERT textbook
teacher_tip: Reinforce sign convention consistently from the beginning. Use plenty of ray diagrams to build visualization skills. Encourage students to relate every concept to at least one daily-life example to improve retention and interest.
